data.title: OKX to Launch ZBT (ZEROBASE) Pre-Market Trading
data.description: According to official news, OKX will launch pre-market trading of ZBT (ZEROBASE) at 7:30 PM (UTC+8) on October 17, 2025. ZEROBASE is a decentralized cryptographic infrastructure network that enables verifiable off-chain computations through zero-knowledge proofs and a trusted execution environment.

data.title: Tether Releases Open-Source Wallet Development Kit
data.description: According to Foresight News, Tether has announced the release of its open-source Wallet Development Kit (WDK). This new toolkit is designed to empower humans, machines, and AI agents to build, deploy, and use secure, multi-chain, self-custodial wallets. These wallets can be integrated into any device, ranging from the smallest embedded systems to mobile, desktop, or server operating systems.

data.title: Zcash price drops to $190 amid broader crypto pullback
data.description:
Zcash price dropped to the $190 support level. Macro headwinds also had Bitcoin falling to below $105,000 to trigger further bleeding across crypto. Analysts remain bullish despite the dip. Zcash (ZEC) tumbled to lows of $190, with its double-digit declines reflecting widespread market unease
source: https://coinjournal.net/news/zcash-price-drops-to-190-amid-broader-crypto-pullback/

data.title: Bitcoin Market Shows Resilience Amid Structural Changes
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Cryptoquant analysts have observed that the current market is facing challenges similar to those in 2021, but with a different structural composition. Unlike previous cycles where panic led to increased reserves on trading platforms and amplified selling pressure, the current Bitcoin balance on exchanges is at its lowest in a decade, indicating limited supply available for sale and a tighter market structure. The reduction in Bitcoin held by exchanges suggests that a sustained downward trend is unlikely.

Long-term holders are behaving differently compared to past periods. During 2020 and 2021, the Long-Term Holder Spent Output Profit Ratio (LTH-SOPR) remained below 1 for several months, indicating potential losses for investors fearing a market exit. However, during the recent downturn, this ratio has stayed near neutral, reflecting cautious profit-taking rather than fear-driven selling. Long-term investors continue to hold through volatility, enhancing market resilience.

Looking back at previous shocks, the March 2020 crash cleared leverage, leading to significant accumulation by large holders and a V-shaped recovery. In May 2021, Bitcoin fell 30% due to pressure from Tesla and regulatory actions, with large holders selling approximately 50,000 Bitcoins and later repurchasing 34,000 at the bottom. In August 2023, the U.S. debt rating downgrade triggered a 15% pullback, with SOPR briefly dropping before quickly rebounding. Each event cleared excessive leverage and initiated a new accumulation phase.

The current market downturn appears more mature, with reduced exchange reserves and stable long-term holders. Temporary volatility does not equate to structural weakness, as Bitcoin is laying the groundwork for the next upward cycle.

data.title: Bitcoin's Relative Strength Index Indicates Oversold Status Against Gold
data.description:
According to Foresight News, CoinDesk analyst Omkar Godbole reports that Bitcoin's 14-day relative strength index (RSI) against gold is currently at its most oversold level. The RSI has dropped to 22.20, slightly below February's low, reaching levels not seen since November 2022. Typically, an RSI reading below 30 is considered oversold, suggesting that Bitcoin has experienced significant selling pressure relative to gold, potentially undervaluing the ratio between the two.

However, an oversold RSI alone does not guarantee an immediate bullish reversal for Bitcoin against gold. Confirmation from other technical indicators is necessary, such as signs of a downtrend exhaustion, bullish divergence, or increased buying volume. Without these supporting signals, the oversold condition may persist in a strong downtrend, indicating that despite the low RSI, prices could continue to decline. Given the bearish technical backdrop, Bitcoin bulls need to remain patient and wait for clearer signs of a trend reversal before expecting a sustained recovery.

Meanwhile, the 14-day RSI for BTC/USD has not yet reached the oversold territory, and the MACD histogram continues to form deeper bars below the oversold region, indicating that selling pressure may persist. The price has been confirmed below the 200-day moving average, which could trigger more momentum traders to sell. Data shows that the 50-week simple moving average (SMA), currently around $101,700, remains a key support level for Bitcoin. Since the bull market began in early 2023, this moving average has provided reliable support, helping prices reach new highs.
